ddls:
CREATE TABLE "employees" (
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employee */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the employee */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the employee */,
  "job_title_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the job_titles table */,
  "hourly_rate" REAL /* Hourly wage of the employee */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the department the employee belongs to */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the employee */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the employee */,
  "address" TEXT /* Street address of the employee */,
  "city" TEXT /* City of the employee's address */,
  "state" TEXT /* State of the employee's address */,
  "zip" TEXT /* Zip code of the employee's address */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_job_title_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_title_id") REFERENCES job_titles ("title_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the department's functions */,
  "budget" REAL /* Total budget allocated to the department */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "payroll_records" (
  "payroll_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each payroll record */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee receiving the payment */,
  "pay_period_start" TEXT /* Start date of the pay period */,
  "pay_period_end" TEXT /* End date of the pay period */,
  "total_hours" REAL /* Total hours worked in the pay period */,
  "total_pay" REAL /* Total pay issued for the pay period */,
  "payroll_status" TEXT /* Status of the payroll record (e.g., 'processed', 'pending', 'rejected') */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("payroll_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_payroll_records_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_titles" (
  "title_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job title */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Name of the job title */,
  "classification" TEXT /* Classification of the job (e.g., Full-time, Part-time) */,
  "base_hourly_rate" REAL /* Base hourly rate for the job title */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("title_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "salary_history" (
  "salary_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each salary change record */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee whose salary was changed */,
  "effective_date" TEXT /* Date when the salary change became effective */,
  "old_rate" REAL /* Previous hourly rate before the change */,
  "new_rate" REAL /* New hourly rate after the change */,
  "reason" TEXT /* Reason for the salary change (e.g., 'promotion', 'cost of living adjustment') */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("salary_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_salary_history_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., Admin, HR Manager) */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Descriptions of the permissions granted to this role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user account */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the roles table */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_evaluations" (
  "eval_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance evaluation record */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee being evaluated */,
  "evaluation_date" TEXT /* Date when the performance evaluation was conducted */,
  "rating" TEXT /* Performance rating (e.g., 'exceeds expectations', 'meets expectations') */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Comments or feedback from the evaluator */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("eval_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_evaluations_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "benefits" (
  "benefit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each benefit record */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee receiving the benefit */,
  "benefit_type" TEXT /* Type of benefit (e.g., health insurance, retirement plan) */,
  "benefit_amount" REAL /* Amount of the benefit */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("benefit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_benefits_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)
